title: Gastrochilus patinatus
intro: Gastrochilus patinatus, also known as the Himalayan orchid, is a species of orchid native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. This plant is valued for its traditional medicinal uses, particularly in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine, where it is believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its potential health benefits in treating various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Gastrochilus patinatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, decoctions, and powders, which are used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory disorders.
taxonomy: Gastrochilus patinatus is a species of orchid. It belongs to the genus Gastrochilus and the family Orchidaceae. The species Gastrochilus patinatus is classified within the subfamily Epidendroideae. This orchid species is native to tropical and subtropical regions, particularly in Asia. Gastrochilus patinatus is often found growing on trees and rocks in its natural habitat.
